recommendation: Avoid scheduled use for >8 weeks unless for high-risk patients (e.g., oral corticosteroids or chronic NSAID use) or erosive esophagitis or Barrett's esophagus or pathological hypersecretory condition
rationale: C. diff infections, fractures, hypomagnesemia; the risk of PPI-associated harms increases with long-term use
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2 (Gastrointestinal), J Am Geriatr Soc 71:2052-2081

recommendation: Avoid scheduled use for >8 weeks unless for high-risk patients or erosive esophagitis or Barrett's esophagus or pathological hypersecretory condition
rationale: C. diff infections, fractures, hypomagnesemia

recommendation: Avoid scheduled use for >8 weeks without documented indication
rationale: C. diff infections, fractures, hypomagnesemia

recommendation: Avoid scheduled use for >8 weeks without documented indication
rationale: C. diff infections, fractures, hypomagnesemia, vitamin B12 deficiency

recommendation: Avoid scheduled use for >8 weeks without documented indication
rationale: C. diff infections, fractures, hypomagnesemia

recommendation: Use with caution. Increased risk of CNS adverse effects including ataxia, dizziness, sedation, and falls.
rationale: Added to 2023 Beers list due to substantial evidence of CNS effects and falls risk in older adults, especially with CKD where gabapentin accumulates
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2 (Central Nervous System); new in 2023

recommendation: Use with caution. Increased risk of CNS adverse effects and falls.
rationale: Same class as gabapentin, similar risk profile; additive CNS depression with opioids and benzodiazepines
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2 (Central Nervous System)

recommendation: Avoid use for primary prevention of CVD or colorectal cancer in adults age 70 and older
rationale: Lack of benefit for primary prevention in older adults and increased risk of major bleeding events (USPSTF 2022 alignment)

recommendation: Avoid (oral); first-generation antihistamines highly anticholinergic
rationale: Highly anticholinergic; clearance reduced with advanced age; tolerance develops rapidly when used as hypnotic; risk of confusion, dry mouth, constipation, urinary retention, and other anticholinergic effects

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Highly anticholinergic, sedating; risk of confusion, falls, and other anticholinergic effects in older adults

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Highly anticholinergic; sedation; risk of confusion and falls

recommendation: Avoid (commonly found in OTC sleep aids and cold remedies)
rationale: Highly anticholinergic; tolerance develops rapidly when used as hypnotic; OTC formulations frequently overlooked in medication reconciliation

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Highly anticholinergic, sedating; extrapyramidal symptoms and orthostatic hypotension

recommendation: Avoid in adults aged ≥65 unless other agents fail; if used, prefer extended-release formulations
rationale: Highly anticholinergic; uncertain effectiveness; high anticholinergic burden contributes to cognitive decline and falls

recommendation: Avoid; contributes to anticholinergic burden
rationale: Anticholinergic effects; uncertain efficacy in older adults; can worsen cognition

recommendation: Avoid; consider mirabegron as alternative for overactive bladder
rationale: Anticholinergic effects increase risk of cognitive impairment and falls

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Anticholinergic burden

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Anticholinergic burden

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Anticholinergic burden; active metabolite same as tolterodine

recommendation: Avoid for any indication (long-acting); does not include benzodiazepine receptor agonists used for procedural sedation
rationale: Older adults have increased sensitivity to benzodiazepines and slower metabolism of long-acting agents; risk of cognitive impairment, delirium, falls, fractures, and motor vehicle accidents

recommendation: Avoid for any indication
rationale: Cognitive impairment, delirium, falls, fractures; high abuse potential

recommendation: Avoid for any indication
rationale: Cognitive impairment, delirium, falls; though shorter-acting, still poses substantial risk in older adults

recommendation: Avoid for any indication (long-acting)
rationale: Long half-life with active metabolites; accumulation in older adults

recommendation: Avoid for any indication
rationale: Same class effects; ED visits and hospitalizations

recommendation: Avoid for any indication
rationale: High potency, anterograde amnesia, falls

recommendation: Avoid for any indication
rationale: Long-acting benzodiazepine; falls, cognitive impairment

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Adverse events similar to those of benzodiazepines in older adults; increased ED visits and hospitalizations; motor vehicle accidents; complex sleep behaviors (FDA black box 2019)
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2 (Z-drugs); FDA Black Box 2019

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Same class as zolpidem; minimal effectiveness vs risk

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Same class; falls and complex sleep behaviors

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Highly anticholinergic, sedating, and cause orthostatic hypotension; risks outweigh benefits in older adults

recommendation: Avoid doses >6mg/day; doses ≤6mg/day for insomnia have minimal anticholinergic effects and may be acceptable
rationale: Doses >6mg/day highly anticholinergic

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Anticholinergic, sedating, orthostatic hypotension

recommendation: Avoid; if needed for neuropathic pain, use lowest dose with caution (less anticholinergic than amitriptyline but still problematic)
rationale: Less anticholinergic than amitriptyline but still risky

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Highly anticholinergic; sedation; orthostatic hypotension

recommendation: Avoid chronic use unless alternatives not effective and patient can take proton pump inhibitor or misoprostol; oral and parenteral forms
rationale: Increased risk of GI bleeding, peptic ulcer disease, and acute kidney injury in older adults; especially risky in CKD and HFrEF

recommendation: Avoid chronic use unless alternatives not effective
rationale: Increased risk of GI bleeding and AKI in older adults

recommendation: Avoid chronic use; highest cardiovascular risk among non-selective NSAIDs
rationale: GI bleeding, AKI, MI risk

recommendation: Avoid; do not use for >5 days at any dose
rationale: Markedly increased risk of GI bleeding and AKI; FDA limits use to ≤5 days

recommendation: Avoid; greatest CNS adverse effects among NSAIDs (confusion, headache)
rationale: Highest CNS toxicity of all NSAIDs; older adults particularly susceptible

recommendation: Avoid chronic use unless alternatives not effective
rationale: Although partially COX-2 selective, still confers GI and renal risk in older adults

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Most muscle relaxants are poorly tolerated by older adults; anticholinergic effects, sedation, increased risk of fractures

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Sedation, anticholinergic effects, falls; questionable efficacy in older adults

recommendation: Avoid; abuse potential
rationale: Metabolized to meprobamate; high abuse and dependence potential

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Sedation, anticholinergic effects

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Sedation, hepatotoxicity, falls

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Higher risk of severe prolonged hypoglycemia in older adults due to renal metabolism of active metabolites

recommendation: Avoid (US name for glibenclamide)
rationale: Long-acting sulfonylurea with active metabolites that accumulate in renal impairment, causing prolonged hypoglycemia. Prefer glipizide IR or non-sulfonylurea agents (DPP-4i, SGLT2i, GLP-1).

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Prolonged half-life in older adults; severe hypoglycemia; SIADH

recommendation: Avoid sliding-scale insulin (SSI) as sole regimen; higher risk of hypoglycemia without improvement in glycemic management
rationale: SSI provides reactive rather than proactive coverage; associated with increased hypoglycemia and hyperglycemia variability in long-term care

recommendation: Avoid; minimal effect on weight and increased risk of thrombotic events and possibly death in older adults
rationale: Increased risk of VTE, fluid retention, adrenal suppression; minimal weight gain in older adults

recommendation: Avoid systemic estrogen (with or without progestin) in postmenopausal women; topical vaginal estrogen for atrophy is acceptable at lowest effective dose
rationale: Carcinogenic potential (breast, endometrium); lack of cardioprotective effect; cognitive effects in older women

recommendation: Avoid unless indicated for confirmed hypogonadism with clinical symptoms
rationale: Cardiovascular risk; potential exacerbation of prostate or cardiac conditions

recommendation: Avoid as first-line therapy for atrial fibrillation or heart failure; if used, avoid doses >0.125mg/day
rationale: Higher doses associated with increased mortality without clinical benefit; narrow therapeutic index; renal clearance reduced in older adults
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2 (Cardiovascular); ACC/AHA HF 2022

recommendation: Avoid in patients with creatinine clearance <30 mL/min; avoid for long-term suppression
rationale: Inadequate urinary concentration in CKD; pulmonary toxicity, hepatotoxicity, peripheral neuropathy with chronic use

recommendation: Avoid as first-line antihypertensive
rationale: High risk of CNS effects (sedation, depression); may cause bradycardia and orthostatic hypotension

recommendation: Avoid as first-line antihypertensive (oral); transdermal acceptable for HTN if tapered carefully
rationale: High risk of CNS effects, bradycardia, orthostatic hypotension; rebound HTN with abrupt discontinuation

recommendation: Avoid as antihypertensive; orthostatic hypotension
rationale: Orthostatic hypotension; not first-line for HTN per ALLHAT trial

recommendation: Avoid as antihypertensive; orthostatic hypotension
rationale: Same class as doxazosin

recommendation: Avoid as antihypertensive; orthostatic hypotension and falls
rationale: Same class effects

recommendation: Avoid immediate-release formulation; potential for hypotension and risk of myocardial ischemia
rationale: Reflex tachycardia and precipitous BP drops with immediate-release

recommendation: Avoid as first-line therapy for atrial fibrillation unless heart failure or substantial LV hypertrophy is present
rationale: Multiple toxicities (pulmonary, thyroid, hepatic, ocular); other agents preferred

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Strong negative inotrope and highly anticholinergic; may induce HF

recommendation: Avoid spironolactone >25mg/day in patients with HF or with eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m²
rationale: Increased risk of hyperkalemia, particularly when combined with ACE inhibitors, ARBs, NSAIDs, or potassium supplements

recommendation: Avoid in patients with dementia or Parkinson's disease; avoid as first-line for behavioral symptoms of dementia
rationale: Increased risk of stroke and mortality in dementia; extrapyramidal symptoms; QT prolongation. Black box warning for dementia-related psychosis.
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2/3 (CNS/Dementia); FDA Black Box

recommendation: Avoid as first-line for behavioral symptoms of dementia unless nonpharmacologic measures fail and patient is a threat to self or others
rationale: Increased risk of stroke and mortality in dementia (FDA black box); EPS; metabolic effects
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2/3 (CNS/Dementia); FDA Black Box

recommendation: Avoid as first-line for behavioral symptoms of dementia
rationale: Stroke and mortality risk in dementia (FDA black box); metabolic effects; sedation and falls
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2/3 (CNS/Dementia); FDA Black Box

recommendation: Avoid as first-line for behavioral symptoms of dementia
rationale: Same class warnings; sedation, orthostatic hypotension, falls
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2/3 (CNS/Dementia); FDA Black Box

recommendation: Avoid; not effective oral analgesic; risk of neurotoxicity and seizures
rationale: Active metabolite normeperidine accumulates in renal impairment, causing tremor, myoclonus, seizures; superior alternatives available

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: More CNS adverse effects (confusion, hallucinations) than other opioids

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Highly anticholinergic; uncertain effectiveness

recommendation: Avoid
rationale: Highly anticholinergic

recommendation: Avoid (oral); transdermal short-term acceptable
rationale: Highly anticholinergic; CNS effects

recommendation: Avoid; tardive dyskinesia and other extrapyramidal side effects more common in older adults; use only if gastroparesis confirmed and limit to ≤12 weeks
rationale: Can cause irreversible tardive dyskinesia (FDA black box); EPS; risk increases with duration
source: AGS 2023 Beers Criteria — Table 2 (GI); FDA Black Box 2009

recommendation: Avoid for treatment of nocturia or nocturnal polyuria
rationale: High risk of hyponatremia; safer alternatives available

recommendation: Avoid; high rate of physical dependence, tolerance to sleep benefits, greater risk of overdose at low dosages
rationale: Tolerance, dependence, overdose risk; superior alternatives for seizures

recommendation: Avoid as first-line; only continue if used for seizure control with no superior alternative
rationale: Sedation, dependence, falls, drug interactions (potent CYP inducer)
